task_completion.description: The agent created a new segment named 'Bladder' within the Segment Editor module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Editor' module is visible in the 3D Slicer interface. In the segment list table, a row labeled 'Bladder' exists.

task_completion.description: The bladder structure is visually segmented in the 2D slice views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Red, Yellow, or Green slice views, a colored overlay (mask) covers the bladder, which appears as a rounded, grey, fluid-filled structure in the midline of the pelvis/lower abdomen.

task_completion.description: The agent calculated the volume of the bladder segment using the Segment Statistics module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Statistics' module is open. The results table shows the 'Bladder' segment with a calculated value in the 'Volume' or 'Volume (cc)' column.

task_completion.description: A JSON report was created containing the volume, distension status, and clinical significance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output displays the content of 'bladder_report.json'. The JSON must contain the keys: 'volume_ml', 'distension_status', and 'clinical_significance'.

task_completion.description: The segmentation file and/or report were saved to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Data' dialog or file explorer window is visible, showing 'bladder_segmentation.nii.gz' or 'bladder_report.json' inside the '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/' folder.

integrity.description: The agent utilized the 3D Slicer GUI modules (Segment Editor, Segment Statistics) to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Slicer application window with relevant modules active, rather than the agent purely using terminal scripts or guessing values.

integrity.description: The classification in the report is consistent with the measured volume based on the task rules.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the volume is visible in the report or statistics table, the 'distension_status' matches the rules: Normal <300, Mild 300-500, Moderate 500-800, Severe >800.

integrity.description: The agent navigated the software naturally to locate the bladder.
integrity.visual_evidence: The slice views show the pelvic region (bladder visible) rather than remaining on the default chest/abdomen view or blank screens.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Classification Rules for verification: Normal (<300mL), Mildly Distended (300-500mL), Moderately Distended (500-800mL), Severely Distended (>800mL). Clinical Significance is true if volume > 500mL.
